key_insight: Cross-audit projection (CAP) is a new bias-correction approach for binary model risk prediction that fixes a weakness of K-fold cross-validation in estimating class-specific risks.
clinical_relevance: For precision oncology models, CAP could provide more reliable risk estimates and uncertainty quantification than standard cross-validation when evaluating classification performance.
method: Uses a two-step procedure: cross-audit to estimate over-optimism on resampled subsamples.
Adds an asymptotic-theory-informed projection to adjust bias for the smaller resample size.
Develops an inference procedure and shows CAP is second-order asymptotically unbiased.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports higher-order asymptotic analysis, simulation studies, and a breast cancer detection application showing favorable finite-sample performance.
watch_item: The method is theory-driven and may require careful implementation and validation in real-world datasets before routine use.
paper_title: Cross-Audit Projection for Model Risk Prediction
